# DejaViewed — Full Catalog (334 entries)

## L1B3RT4S — Pliny jailbreak prompts still landing on GPT-5
- Creator: @_dbrogle | Type: repo | Tier: S | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DOPqBfyDMHG/
- Tools: ChatGPT, GPT-5, elder-plinius/L1B3RT4S | Techniques: jailbreaking, prompt injection | Domains: LLM, prompt engineering, security
- @_dbrogle tests elder-plinius's L1B3RT4S jailbreak repo (github.com/elder-plinius/L1B3RT4S) against GPT-5 and it still works despite tighter safety. Worth revisiting for red-team prompt libraries.

## Platonic Representation Hypothesis — embeddings as AI telepathy
- Creator: @lizthedeveloper | Type: news | Tier: A | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DJ94FEmoMT7/
- Techniques: embeddings, platonic representation hypothesis | Domains: LLM, data science
- @lizthedeveloper riffs on the Platonic Representation Hypothesis paper, framing shared embedding spaces across models as a kind of 'AI telepathy'. Pointer back to the original research paper on vector/embedding convergence.

## OpenAI GPT-5 prompting framework — clarity, context, role
- Creator: @theaifield | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DNTuDtvtZkS/
- Tools: GPT-5, OpenAI | Techniques: context loading, prompt engineering, role prompting | Domains: LLM, prompt engineering
- @theaifield summarizes OpenAI's own GPT-5 prompting guide: be explicit with instructions, load context heavily, assign a role. Carousel-style breakdown; actual value is remembering OpenAI published an official prompt guide.

## GeoSpy.ai — geolocate any photo from buildings, plants, shadows
- Creator: @innovation | Type: tool | Tier: A | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DNS0YG0NrLt/
- Tools: GeoSpy | Domains: data science, image generation, photography
- GeoSpy.ai infers photo location from architectural, botanical and lighting cues against a geotagged image database. Link: https://geospy.ai/ . OSINT/geolocation tool worth bookmarking.

## Build Wars recap — AI gets you 80%, designers finish the 20%
- Creator: @itsdesignertom | Type: design | Tier: B | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DG0-8I7SZCz/
- Domains: design
- @itsdesignertom's newsletter dissecting the Brotzky $100 / Jhey CSS Build Wars showdown. Core lesson: AI nails the first 80% of a UI but handcrafted tweaks still matter. Published via @uxtoolsco.

## Gemini CLI — free terminal coding agent, 1K req/day, Gemini 2.5 Pro
- Creator: @davi.d_roberts | Type: tool | Tier: S | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DLuiACnKWP9/
- Tools: Anthropic, Claude, Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini, Gemini CLI, Google, Veo | Techniques: AI agents, code generation | Domains: LLM, agents, software development
- Google's Gemini CLI: free AI coding agent in the terminal, 1,000 req/day, 1M context on Gemini 2.5 Pro, plus Veo/Imagen generation. Positioned as a Cursor/Claude Code killer.

## JSON image-prompt profiles — parameterized context for ChatGPT
- Creator: @chase.variantai | Type: technique | Tier: B | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DLTpkBSoiwX/
- Tools: ChatGPT | Techniques: JSON context profiles, prompt templating | Domains: LLM, prompt engineering
- @chase.variantai demos using structured JSON 'context profiles' to drive image generation in ChatGPT, with dynamically swappable parameters. Proof-of-concept technique for repeatable AI image prompts.

## Vibe-coded games era — voxel MMO in a day, Claude plays Pokemon
- Creator: @itsdesignertom | Type: news | Tier: B | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DGn-XM2vikd/
- Tools: Claude | Domains: LLM, creative coding
- @itsdesignertom riff on the AI-game-dev moment: a multiplayer voxel game shipped in a day, a banana-clicker sitting at Steam #6, Claude playing an AI-generated Pokemon clone. Cultural snapshot of indie-AI gamedev vs big studios.

## Blender MCP + Tripo — 2D reference image to full 3D scene
- Creator: @bilawal.ai | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DHoPsMOxIRl/
- Tools: Blender, MCP, Tripo | Techniques: 2D-to-3D pipeline, MCP, MCP servers | Domains: 3D, agents, reference
- @bilawal.ai demos a Blender MCP integration that takes a 2D reference image, has an LLM decompose the scene, generates 3D assets via Tripo, and arranges them to match. Forward-looking on Unity/Unreal MCP equivalents.

## MediaPipe TouchDesigner plugin by @blankensmithing
- Creator: @neles0n | Type: demo | Tier: A | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DFaChZeMdn1/
- Tools: MediaPipe, TouchDesigner | Domains: 3D, creative coding
- @neles0n trying the MediaPipe plugin by @blankensmithing inside TouchDesigner. Useful pointer for anyone doing body/hand tracking in TD creative-coding pipelines.

## AI rotoscoping — mask yourself into your own animations
- Creator: @unknown | Type: technique | Tier: B | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DHTvblkRgZG/
- Techniques: segmentation mask rotoscope | Domains: art, image generation, video production
- @iamfesq demos using an AI detection/segmentation model to auto-rotoscope a mask of himself for compositing into his animated art. Quick technique reference for AI-assisted rotoscoping.

## Ollama — run open-source LLMs locally to learn how they work
- Creator: @edhonour | Type: tutorial | Tier: A | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DJM4x16uQMY/
- Tools: Ollama | Domains: LLM
- @edhonour: short PSA that installing Ollama is the easiest way to run open-source models locally and poke at their internals. Baseline local-LLM entry point.

## Qwen 2.5 Max, Wan 2.1, Lumina 2 — China's open-source trio
- Creator: @nick_saraev | Type: tool | Tier: A | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DIaraR1MjLZ/
- Tools: ChatGPT, DeepSeek, Midjourney, Qwen | Domains: LLM
- @nick_saraev walks through three open-source Chinese AI models: Qwen 2.5 Max (chat/code/search/image/video), Wan 2.1 (open-source video gen), and Lumina 2 (photoreal image gen). All free, all downloadable.

## Hunyuan3D-2 — Tencent's free image-to-3D model generator
- Creator: @nick_saraev | Type: tool | Tier: S | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DIcw6N6snq1/
- Tools: Hunyuan3D, Hunyuan3D-2, Tencent | Domains: 3D, image generation
- Tencent's Hunyuan3D-2 turns text or an uploaded image into textured 3D models in seconds. Free web UI; pipeline is upload > Generate Shape > Generate Textured Shape > download. Good for game/app asset workflows.

- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DIe7QoQCong/
- Tools: ChatGPT, Google | Techniques: prompt engineering | Domains: LLM, prompt engineering
- @aitoolhub.co reshares @robonuggets's recap of Google's own prompt engineering guide, pitched as the most comprehensive from a major lab. Pointer to the Google whitepaper.

## 50-neuron MNIST net — visualized prediction mapping (Eric Vyacheslav)
- Creator: @beautiful.digits | Type: demo | Tier: B | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DDxSqstOByh/
- Domains: data science
- Visualization (credit Eric Vyacheslav) of a 2-layer, 50-neuron network predicting handwritten digits, showing live activation/prediction mapping. Nice teaching/visual reference.

## Kling Motion Brush — direct up to 5 characters in one AI video
- Creator: @realrileybrown | Type: tool | Tier: A | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DDFdSEDtKZV/
- Tools: Kling, Midjourney | Techniques: motion brush | Domains: image generation, video production
- @realrileybrown on Kling's Motion Brush: select up to 5 characters/objects in a starting image (often Midjourney-generated) and draw motion paths to animate them. One of the sharpest controllable video-gen workflows at the time.

## Runway non-linear video — node canvas of AI keyframes
- Creator: @realrileybrown | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DDIAME6MhzY/
- Tools: Runway | Techniques: keyframe graph editing, node-based editing, node-based video | Domains: video production
- @realrileybrown explains Runway's 'non-linear AI video exploration': work on a canvas of node-connected keyframes, let AI interpolate between them, branch alternate universes instead of a linear timeline.

## World Labs — explorable 3D worlds from a single 2D image
- Creator: @rpn | Type: tool | Tier: S | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DDF0mC1vvkh/
- Tools: World Labs | Domains: 3D, image generation
- @rpn covers World Labs' first demo: generate navigable 3D worlds from one image or sentence, with camera controls and style effects. Referenced as a likely Quest/Vision Pro use case.

## Striking-distance keywords — GSC SEO tactic (positions 6-11)
- Creator: @edward.builds | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/C9F2Tx_iii0/
- Tools: Google, Google Search Console | Techniques: striking-distance keywords | Domains: web
- @edward.builds SEO tactic: in Google Search Console, filter for keywords ranking at positions 6-11, add a few sentences of relevant copy to the page, often jumps to page 1. Concrete, low-effort SEO play.

## AI-styled QR codes — generate codes that look like images
- Creator: @setupspawn | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/C6JRjOVAOf-/
- Techniques: ControlNet QR conditioning | Domains: art, image generation
- @setupspawn demos a tool where you supply a URL or QR + a prompt and it generates a QR code stylized as an image, still scannable. Creative-marketing technique.

## Sketch-to-3D-anim pipeline — Procreate + Magnific + 3DAI + Move AI
- Creator: @chatgptricks | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: ai1
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/C5010-Kh1Mv/
- Tools: 3DAI Studio, ChatGPT, Magnific, Magnific AI, Move AI, Procreate | Techniques: markerless mocap, sketch-to-3D, style transfer | Domains: 3D, art, video production
- Director Jon Finger's pipeline: Procreate sketches -> Magnific AI style transfer -> 3DAI Studio for 3D models -> Move AI for mocap -> Cinema 4D for render. Concrete multi-tool AI animation workflow.

## Boris Cherny's Claude Code workflow — parallel worktrees + plan mode
- Creator: @evolving.ai | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: ai2
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVQ5o6YDfsp/
- Tools: Claude, Claude Code | Techniques: parallel git worktrees, subagent orchestration, workflow automation | Domains: LLM, agents
- @evolving.ai summarizes Boris Cherny's X thread on how the Anthropic team actually uses Claude Code: 3-5 git worktrees in parallel, start every complex task in plan mode, update rules after every mistake, build reusable skills and subagents.

## Anthropic blocks Claude Max subscription tokens in 3rd-party tools
- Creator: @tech.unicorn | Type: tool | Tier: A | Collection: ai2
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVKJlJYEsmm/
- Tools: Anthropic, Claude, Claude Max, DeepSeek, Gemini, Llama, Notion, Open Claw | Domains: agents, automation
- @tech.unicorn on Anthropic killing subscription-token access in third-party tools (Jan 9 ToS change), spiking effective cost from $200/mo to $500-$2000/mo on API. Suggests multi-model routers (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/GPT-4o) as the mitigation.

## DATA CONTROL — Kinect + TouchDesigner + Unreal scientific viz
- Creator: @pala3d | Type: demo | Tier: A | Collection: ai2
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVoReJNCGxT/
- Tools: Kinect, TouchDesigner, Unreal Engine | Domains: 3D, creative coding, data science
- @pala3d's 'DATA CONTROL' project: real-time visualization of NASA/CERN datasets as particle systems, body-controlled via Kinect through TouchDesigner into Unreal Engine. Pipeline explicitly human-made, no AI. Reference for data-viz/creative-coding stack.

## Boris Cherny's personal CLAUDE.md — plan-first, self-improving rules
- Creator: @keshavsuki | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: ai2
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DV6QY8QjoRW/
- Tools: Anthropic, Claude, Claude Code | Techniques: CLAUDE.md authoring, subagent offloading | Domains: LLM, agents
- @keshavsuki dissects Boris Cherny's (Anthropic) personal CLAUDE.md: plan-first, subagents for complex work, self-improving lessons.md loop, hard verification standard, elegance demand, autonomous bug fixing, git discipline. High-signal template for CLAUDE.md design.

- Tools: Claude, Claude Code | Domains: UI/UX, agents
- @toddybuilds tip: if Claude feels overwhelming, ask Claude to generate a dashboard that summarizes what it's doing. Light but useful meta-prompt idea.

## Open-source geopolitics terminal — OSINT feeds + AI instability scores

- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVuULwWjApN/
- Domains: OSINT, agents, data science
- @brahada_irl teasing a newly open-sourced 'Bloomberg Terminal for geopolitics': real-time global map with military flights/ships, OSINT news scanning across 100+ sources, automated instability scores, dark-ship detection, prediction-market signals. Comment-gated link.

## primer.md + memory.sh — live-updating CLAUDE.md companion
- Creator: @keshavsuki | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: ai2
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVzvsgijOLT/
- Tools: Claude, Claude Code | Techniques: end-of-session hook, primer.md live session state | Domains: agents
- @keshavsuki on fixing CLAUDE.md staleness: add a primer.md that Claude rewrites at end of every session (current state, next step, blockers), plus a memory.sh launch hook that injects last 5 commits and modified files, plus a git hook logging commits. Four-layer always-current context.

- @stevearnesonofficial teaser: setup guide for wiring Claude Code into NotebookLM. Comment-gated, content itself thin — note only.

- @nateherkai teaser for a YouTube breakdown of his best tips for hitting and managing Claude Code usage limits. Real content lives on YouTube.

## Turboquant — memory backend for Claude/Open Claw AI stores

## Shepherd dog flock simulation — pressure-field + boids rules
- Creator: @simulateitnow | Type: demo | Tier: B | Collection: ai4
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVndfhOE7A9/
- Tools: Arc | Domains: creative coding, data science
- @simulateitnow explains a flock-herding simulation combining a pressure-field force F=(1-d/R)^1.2 with classic boids (cohesion/alignment/separation). Great reference for swarm/multi-agent simulation math.

- Domains: LLM, agents
- @angus.sewell argues that most people are still running local memory stacks and that custom cloud-based LLM 'brains' are about to become a big category. Directional take, no specific tool.

- Techniques: Fama-French factors, Kalman filter, Kalman filters, statistical arbitrage | Domains: data science, finance/trading

## Ant Colony Optimization sim — pheromone feedback + diffusion PDE
- Creator: @simulateitnow | Type: demo | Tier: A | Collection: ai4
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVCBNQ8jCly/
- Techniques: ACO, stigmergy | Domains: creative coding, data science
- @simulateitnow explains ACO / stigmergy with the diffusion-evaporation equation ∂τ/∂t = Δτ + D∇²τ - ρτ, showing how short paths win through faster pheromone reinforcement. Great visual reference for swarm optimization.

## Crypto dollar-neutral reversal — naive long/short, eaten by fees

- Techniques: dollar-neutral reversal, transaction cost analysis | Domains: crypto, finance/trading
- @avi_mukesh: simple reversal strategy on crypto (short if up, long if down), dollar-neutral scaling. Looks good without costs, transaction fees kill it. Honest starter example for learning backtesting caveats.

## STE.GG — open-source AI steganography toolkit (hide in anything)
- Creator: @marc.kaz | Type: tool | Tier: A | Collection: ai4
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DWnHQs-jqr3/
- Techniques: steganography | Domains: automation, data science
- @marc.kaz on STE.GG: open-source steganography platform that hides payloads in images, audio, text, PDFs, packets, ZIPs, emojis. Built-in AI agent auto-tries 120+ LSB combinations, DCT, PVD, chroma, palette, etc. Both reveal and conceal modes.

## 10 non-cliche quant research projects — Bayesian BL, RL factors, PBO
- Creator: @heidioftheeast | Type: tutorial | Tier: S | Collection: ai4

- Techniques: Bayesian Black-Litterman, backtest overfitting detection | Domains: data science, finance/trading
- @heidioftheeast's 10 quant project ideas beyond Black-Scholes: probability of backtest overfitting, Bayesian Black-Litterman, survival-based risk, RL factor portfolios, Sharpe statistics, convex portfolio trading, TS vs XS factors, numeraire-invariant hedging, exotic option model risk, regime-switching factors.

## 10 institutional quant projects — Avellaneda-Stoikov, Hawkes, HMM
- Creator: @heidioftheeast | Type: tutorial | Tier: S | Collection: ai4
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DUytAorCGE_/
- Tools: Meta | Techniques: Almgren-Chriss, Avellaneda-Stoikov MM, Hawkes process | Domains: data science, finance/trading
- @heidioftheeast's deeper 10-project list: Avellaneda-Stoikov market maker, multivariate Hawkes order-flow, Almgren-Chriss cross-impact, eigenportfolios via random matrix theory, realized-kernel vol forecasting, meta-labeling, orthogonal alpha stacking, HMM regime detection, adversarial stress tests, capacity/impact scaling. Serious portfolio builder.

- Tools: GitReverse.com | Domains: agents, prompt engineering

## Gabriel's Horn / Torricelli's Trumpet — finite volume, infinite area
- Creator: @mathxmatrix | Type: inspiration | Tier: C | Collection: ai4
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DUBcUuDCE3j/
- Domains: education
- @mathxmatrix explains Gabriel's Horn (Torricelli, 1643): rotate y=1/x around the x-axis to get a shape with finite volume but infinite surface area. Credit @jeedecodee.

## Ising Model — modeling herd behavior and market phase transitions
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTsn_IKE06E/
- Tools: Python | Techniques: Agent-Based Modeling, Phase Transitions, Symmetry Breaking | Domains: finance/trading, physics, quant
- 3D lattice visualization of the Ising model applied to financial markets. Blue=sellers, orange=buyers. At high volatility the market is random; as social pressure increases, clusters form leading to spontaneous symmetry breaking (crashes/trends). Built in Python for agent-based modeling.

## Phase Space Reconstruction — unfolding hidden BTC volatility dynamics
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTLkCVXE7O6/
- Tools: Python | Techniques: Attractor Analysis, Phase Space Reconstruction, Time Delay Embedding | Domains: chaos-theory, crypto, quant
- Uses Phase Space Reconstruction (PSR) with time delay tau=3 to unfold BTCUSD volatility into a multi-dimensional manifold. Reveals whether market is in stable regime or chaotic transition. Key for quantitative regime detection.

## Heston Model — stochastic volatility for realistic options pricing
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DW6uAQdk1r0/
- Techniques: Mean Reversion, Stochastic Volatility, Volatility Smile, stochastic modeling | Domains: derivatives, options, quant
- Explains why Black-Scholes fails (constant vol assumption) and how the Heston Model introduces stochastic volatility. Captures the volatility smile and skew that basic models miss. Essential for options pricing.

## Cox Process — doubly stochastic Poisson for bursty market events
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DW4GU9HE8nd/
- Techniques: Doubly Stochastic Poisson, Intensity Modeling, Point Processes, stochastic modeling | Domains: quant, stochastic-processes
- Standard Poisson processes assume constant event rates, but markets are bursty. The Cox Process (Doubly Stochastic Poisson) uses a CIR-driven random intensity to model trade arrivals, defaults, and volatility spikes more realistically.

## Hidden Markov Models — detecting market regime shifts automatically
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DW1kTMcExSj/
- Techniques: Regime Detection, State Estimation, Viterbi Algorithm | Domains: finance/trading, ml, quant
- Uses HMMs to mathematically deduce hidden market states from observable returns and volatility. Identifies low-vol bull, high-vol bear, and recovery regimes. Better than lagging indicators for strategy selection.

## Avellaneda-Stoikov — optimal spread calculation for market makers
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DWzAGZzE9gV/
- Tools: Python | Techniques: Inventory Management, Optimal Market Making, Stochastic Control | Domains: hft, market-making, quant
- The Avellaneda-Stoikov model solves the stochastic control problem for market making. Calculates optimal bid-ask spread based on volatility, time horizon, and inventory. Foundation of modern HFT market making. DM-bait for code repo.

## Ornstein-Uhlenbeck Process — mean reversion for pairs trading
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVyXe17E2iz/
- Techniques: Mean Reversion, Pairs Trading, Stochastic Differential Equations, algorithmic trading, mean reversion | Domains: finance/trading, quant, stochastic-processes
- Simulates the OU process showing mean reversion dynamics with theta (reversion speed), mu (equilibrium), and sigma (noise). Core model for pairs trading, interest rate spreads, and volatility signals in quantitative finance.

## Wave Function Collapse analogy — modeling pre-event price uncertainty
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: B | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVTjJW9E1nL/
- Techniques: Event-Driven Trading, Probability Distribution, Tail Risk Modeling | Domains: physics, quant, risk
- Draws analogy between quantum wave function collapse and price discovery after news events. Pre-event price is a probability distribution; the event collapses it to a single reality. Emphasizes modeling uncertainty and tail risks for systematic trading.

## 3D Yield Curve Surface — visualizing cross-asset macro regimes
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVL5slFE51o/
- Techniques: Cross-Asset Correlation, Macro Regime Analysis, Yield Curve Modeling | Domains: 3D, fixed-income, macro, quant
- 3D visualization of yield curve dynamics across macro regimes: COVID-19 vol shock (VIX spike, yield compression), Fed tightening cycle (curve inversion). Shows how Treasury yield warping drives cross-asset correlations.

## Volatility Surface — regime shifts from smile to smirk
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVEU6cyjCaq/
- Techniques: Implied Volatility Surface, Tail Risk Detection, Volatility Smile/Smirk | Domains: options, quant, volatility
- 3D visualization of implied volatility surface (moneyness x maturity x IV). Shows how the surface warps from gentle 'smile' to steep 'smirk' before tail-risk events as markets bid up OTM puts for downside protection.

## Omori's Law — earthquake aftershock math applied to market crashes
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DU8m2RCE1Ea/
- Techniques: Aftershock Modeling, Power Law Decay, Volatility Clustering | Domains: physics, quant, volatility
- Applies geophysics' Omori's Law to financial markets. Post-crash volatility aftershocks decay at a predictable rate following power-law dynamics, not randomly. Shows March 2020 crash decay pattern. DM-bait for code repo.

## Wasserstein Distance — measuring the backtest-to-live reality gap
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DUx9t_FE0CY/
- Techniques: Distribution Shift Detection, Optimal Transport, Sinkhorn Divergence, Wasserstein Distance | Domains: backtesting, ml, quant
- Uses Wasserstein Distance and Sinkhorn Divergence to mathematically measure drift between backtest distributions (blue) and live market conditions (orange). Detects distribution shift that kills more strategies than bad fees.

## Singular Spectrum Analysis — non-parametric trend extraction from noisy data
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DUlVcRVE8xv/
- Techniques: Non-Parametric Decomposition, Singular Spectrum Analysis, Trend Extraction | Domains: quant, signal processing
- SSA decomposes time-series into trend, cycles, and noise without assuming a fixed functional form. Breaks data into reconstructed components (RC 1+7 trend, RC 2+3 annual, RC 9 high-freq). DM-bait for code repo.

## Lempel-Ziv Complexity — compression algorithms to detect market predictability
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DUTQPorEwuP/
- Techniques: Information Theory, Lempel-Ziv Complexity, Pattern Detection | Domains: information-theory, quant
- Applies the LZ compression algorithm (behind .zip files) to financial time series. When LZ complexity is low, patterns repeat and the market is more predictable. When high, it's random noise. Novel cross-domain application.

## Fast Fourier Transform — decomposing NVDA price into frequency signals
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: B | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DUBaBX0kxSA/
- Techniques: Fast Fourier Transform, Frequency Decomposition, Spectral Analysis | Domains: quant, signal processing
- Applies FFT to NVDA price action to decompose it into frequency components, separating noise from dominant cycles. Shows how to identify the strongest periodic signals in price data.

## Monte Carlo — 25,000 shuffled BTC returns to separate edge from luck
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: B | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DT8BX8vEzHm/
- Techniques: Monte Carlo, Monte Carlo Simulation, Permutation Testing, Statistical Significance | Domains: backtesting, crypto, quant
- Runs 25,000 Monte Carlo simulations by shuffling daily BTC-USD returns to test whether a strategy's performance is genuine edge or statistical luck. One backtest isn't enough — real quants demand statistical significance.

## Minimum Spanning Tree — graph theory reveals S&P 500 hidden structure
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DT3Ig_-E7DW/
- Tools: Python | Techniques: Correlation Networks, Graph Theory, Minimum Spanning Tree, Network Analysis | Domains: graph-theory, quant, risk
- Applies graph theory (Minimum Spanning Tree) to the S&P 500 to map the hidden network structure between stocks. Reveals sector clusters and systemic risk connections that traditional correlation matrices obscure. DM-bait for code.

## Avalanche Model — self-organized criticality explains market crashes
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTx3Bi3E-hp/
- Techniques: Cascading Failures, Critical State Analysis, Sandpile Dynamics | Domains: physics, quant, risk
- Markets reach self-organized criticality where small perturbations trigger cascading failures. The 'news' doesn't cause the crash — the system was already at a critical state. Applies sandpile/avalanche dynamics from physics to explain market fragility.

## Tick Data vs OHLC — you're trading on 0.002% of available data
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: inspiration | Tier: B | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTnQ72fk2fm/
- Techniques: LOB Analysis, Market Microstructure, Tick Data Analysis, algorithmic trading | Domains: finance/trading, market-microstructure, quant
- Daily OHLC candles compress ~86,400 seconds of market data into 4 numbers — throwing away 99.998% of information. Makes the case for tick-level and limit order book analysis over candle-based trading.

## Lyapunov Exponent — measuring chaos in BTC tick-level microstructure
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTiLFogE4KT/
- Techniques: Chaos Theory, Lyapunov Exponent, Microstructure Analysis | Domains: chaos-theory, crypto, quant
- Visualizes the Lyapunov Exponent of BTC-USD microstructure data. Positive exponent = chaotic/unpredictable, negative = deterministic. Reveals that what looks random on 5-min charts has measurable chaotic structure at tick level.

## Kalman Filter — adaptive signal extraction for non-stationary markets
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTfqcYUE2uV/
- Techniques: Adaptive Filtering, Kalman filter, Non-Stationary Signal Processing, State Estimation | Domains: quant, signal processing
- Financial data is non-linear and non-stationary, breaking standard filters. Kalman Filter adapts in real-time, unlike lagging moving averages or FFT (which assumes stationarity). Optimal for real-time signal extraction.

## Continuous Wavelet Transform — time-frequency analysis of QQQ
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: B | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTdWruOE1sm/
- Techniques: Continuous Wavelet Transform, Scalogram, Time-Frequency Analysis | Domains: quant, signal processing
- Applies CWT to QQQ to analyze frequency-domain dynamics over time. Unlike FFT (fixed window), wavelets capture how dominant frequencies shift across regimes. Reveals the market's changing 'heartbeat'.

## Random Matrix Theory — cleaning correlation matrices from noise
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: S | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTXv8W_k-0x/
- Techniques: Eigenvalue Filtering, Marcenko-Pastur Distribution, Random Matrix Theory | Domains: linear-algebra, quant, risk
- Standard correlation matrices treat random noise as valid connections. Random Matrix Theory (RMT) filters eigenvalues to 'clean' the matrix, keeping only statistically significant correlations. The market is 90% noise.

## Stable Distribution — modeling fat-tailed crashes beyond Gaussian
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: B | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTSn31Ek2DB/
- Techniques: Extreme Value Theory, Fat Tail Modeling, Non-Gaussian Returns | Domains: quant, risk, statistics
- Markets go up slowly but crash fast. Standard Gaussian models underestimate crash probability. Uses Stable (Levy) distributions to properly model fat tail events and Black Swan risks that beginners ignore.

## Hurst Exponent — 3D surface of BTC memory and mean-reversion zones
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTPrgWIk4Ns/
- Techniques: Fractal Analysis, Hurst exponent, Long-Range Dependence, Rescaled Range Analysis | Domains: 3D, crypto, quant, statistics
- Plots the Hurst Exponent of BTC-USD as a 3D surface. H>0.5 = trending (momentum), H<0.5 = mean-reverting, H=0.5 = random walk. Shows how market 'memory' varies across timeframes. Basis for adaptive strategy selection.

## VectorBT — vectorized backtesting framework explained simply
- Creator: @quant.traderr | Type: technique | Tier: A | Collection: quant
- URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTAjTaBADAx/
- Tools: Python, VectorBT | Techniques: Vectorized Backtesting, backtesting | Domains: backtesting, python, quant
- Brief explainer of VectorBT, a Python backtesting library that uses vectorized operations for fast strategy testing. Aimed at retail traders transitioning to quantitative methods.

- Awesome work design by @kadlab — @inspirationsl0g0; uses Illustrator.

# Deep Dives — Curated Insight Connections

## Natural Language → 3D Scenes: The Pipeline That Didn't Exist 6 Months Ago
- Class: emergent_capability
- Quality: 5/5
- Difficulty: Medium
- Thesis: MCP + Blender + Tripo + Claude Code together create a natural language 3D scene builder — describe what you want, get a full 3D scene. This was impossible before MCP existed.
- Entries: DHoPsMOxIRl, DIcw6N6snq1, DXKFrnQEfEQ, DFaChZeMdn1
- Action: Set up Blender MCP server → connect to Claude Code → use Tripo/Hunyuan3D for mesh gen → describe scenes in plain English → get rigged, lit 3D environments. Add TouchDesigner for live interaction.

## The Autonomous Brand Machine: Claude Code as Your Entire Marketing Team
- Class: workflow_multiplier
- Quality: 5/5
- Difficulty: Hard
- Thesis: Claude Code + Playwright MCP + Higgsfield + knowledge graphs = a system that generates ads, UGC, 3D content, and posts it — autonomously. One person runs what used to need a 5-person team.
- Entries: DXKFrnQEfEQ, DW4OC7qDj_w, DW2J0eUDKHM, DVfZdeqDvyl, DWo1P-KDpMf
- Action: Build Obsidian knowledge base of brand assets → connect via Graphify → Claude Code generates ads/UGC via Higgsfield → Playwright MCP posts to social → iOS Shortcuts for mobile triggers. Total cost: Claude subscription.

## Physics Models → Market Alpha: When Scientists Become Traders
- Class: arbitrage_opportunity
- Quality: 5/5
- Difficulty: Hard
- Thesis: 26 posts map physics/math models to trading — avalanche theory, Ising models, phase space reconstruction. The meta-insight: the quant edge isn't in finance, it's in importing models from other fields.
- Entries: DTLkCVXE7O6, DTx3Bi3E-hp, DTsn_IKE06E, DW6uAQdk1r0, DU8m2RCE1Ea, DUx9t_FE0CY, DT-rdimDamA, DVi7KgKjZbM
- Action: Study the cross-disciplinary models. Build a signal ensemble that combines 3+ physics-derived indicators. Backtest with Monte Carlo validation. Deploy as an AI agent that runs daily.

## The Self-Improving AI Workspace: Claude Code That Gets Smarter Every Session
- Class: life_leverage
- Quality: 5/5
- Difficulty: Medium
- Thesis: CLAUDE.md + primer.md + memory hooks + Obsidian + parallel worktrees = an AI dev environment that learns your preferences, remembers context, and runs multiple streams simultaneously.
- Entries: DV6QY8QjoRW, DVQ5o6YDfsp, DVzvsgijOLT, DXCu2p4EQe3, DWo1P-KDpMf, DWoxW7pjjkH
- Action: Set up CLAUDE.md with self-improvement rules → add primer.md for session continuity → connect Obsidian vault → enable parallel worktrees for concurrent work → watch the system compound over weeks.

## Napkin Sketch → Production Asset: The 5-Tool Creative Pipeline
- Class: creative_fusion
- Quality: 4/5
- Difficulty: Easy
- Thesis: A hand-drawn sketch can become a production-ready 3D animation in under an hour by chaining Procreate → Magnific AI → 3DAI Studio → Move AI → final render. Each tool handles one transformation step.
- Entries: C5010-Kh1Mv, DDFdSEDtKZV, DIcw6N6snq1, DHTvblkRgZG
- Action: Draw in Procreate → upscale with Magnific → generate 3D mesh with Hunyuan3D or 3DAI → capture motion with Move AI → composite with Kling or Runway. Total time: ~45 minutes per asset.

## Body → Machine → Art: Real-Time Interactive Installations Anyone Can Build
- Class: expression_amplifier
- Quality: 4/5
- Difficulty: Medium
- Thesis: Kinect/MediaPipe body tracking + TouchDesigner + Unreal Engine = interactive art installations that respond to human movement. Previously needed a studio budget; now runs on a gaming laptop.
- Entries: DVoReJNCGxT, DFaChZeMdn1, DW9OgzCFcGY, CvsDXJbJh6O, CwNnre_Ih9l
- Action: MediaPipe for body tracking (free, runs on webcam) → pipe data to TouchDesigner → drive visuals in Unreal or project with a $200 projector. Gallery-quality interactive art for under $500 in hardware.

## The One-Person Video Studio: AI Tools That Replace a Production Team
- Class: workflow_multiplier
- Quality: 4/5
- Difficulty: Easy
- Thesis: Runway + Kling + rotoscoping + motion brush = professional video production without a crew, studio, or actors. Direct scenes with text prompts and motion controls.
- Entries: DDIAME6MhzY, DDFdSEDtKZV, C_8ia4lsMB3, C91jKVfxtG1, C7AUmaYS_tc, DHTvblkRgZG, DWl3Bo6jqX1
- Action: Script with Claude → generate base footage with Runway/Kling → direct characters with motion brush → rotoscope yourself in → edit on the node canvas → export. Cost: ~$30/month in AI tools.

## Code as Canvas: When Algorithms Become the Artist
- Class: expression_amplifier
- Quality: 4/5
- Difficulty: Medium
- Thesis: Ant colonies, boid simulations, particle systems, and generative algorithms create art that's alive — emergent beauty from simple rules. The artist writes the rules, not the pixels.
- Entries: DVCBNQ8jCly, DVndfhOE7A9, DVzqCC0jfH4, DWTTnvAgik_, DWLMdCUN1eR, DWlrC88Db2r
- Action: Pick a natural system (flocking, diffusion, growth). Code it in p5.js or TouchDesigner. Add interaction (sound, body tracking, data feeds). The art emerges — you don't draw it, you evolve it.

## L1B3RT4S — Pliny jailbreak prompts still landing on GPT-5
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @_dbrogle tests elder-plinius's L1B3RT4S jailbreak repo (github.com/elder-plinius/L1B3RT4S) against GPT-5 and it still works despite tighter safety. Worth revisiting for red-team prompt libraries.
- Entries: DOPqBfyDMHG, DNTuDtvtZkS, DLTpkBSoiwX, DIaraR1MjLZ, DIe7QoQCong, C_iwddVPU0G

## Gemini CLI — free terminal coding agent, 1K req/day, Gemini 2.5 Pro
- Class: guide
- Thesis: Google's Gemini CLI: free AI coding agent in the terminal, 1,000 req/day, 1M context on Gemini 2.5 Pro, plus Veo/Imagen generation. Positioned as a Cursor/Claude Code killer.
- Entries: DLuiACnKWP9, DGn-XM2vikd, DIe7QoQCong, DBS0RmsR3zZ, C_iwddVPU0G, C8_7jdUssQH

## Blender MCP + Tripo — 2D reference image to full 3D scene
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @bilawal.ai demos a Blender MCP integration that takes a 2D reference image, has an LLM decompose the scene, generates 3D assets via Tripo, and arranges them to match. Forward-looking on Unity/Unreal MCP equivalents.
- Entries: DHoPsMOxIRl, DUzg0n1jRK-, DWncXNCD07J, DWr2krjEgoe, DWo1P-KDpMf, DOSBEmJD5nw

## Boris Cherny's Claude Code workflow — parallel worktrees + plan mode
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @evolving.ai summarizes Boris Cherny's X thread on how the Anthropic team actually uses Claude Code: 3-5 git worktrees in parallel, start every complex task in plan mode, update rules after every mistake, build reusable skills and subagents.
- Entries: DVQ5o6YDfsp, DLuiACnKWP9, DGn-XM2vikd, C_iwddVPU0G, C8_7jdUssQH, DVKJlJYEsmm

## Boris Cherny's personal CLAUDE.md — plan-first, self-improving rules
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @keshavsuki dissects Boris Cherny's (Anthropic) personal CLAUDE.md: plan-first, subagents for complex work, self-improving lessons.md loop, hard verification standard, elegance demand, autonomous bug fixing, git discipline. High-signal template for CLAUDE.md design.
- Entries: DV6QY8QjoRW, DLuiACnKWP9, DGn-XM2vikd, DBS0RmsR3zZ, C_iwddVPU0G, C8_7jdUssQH

## primer.md + memory.sh — live-updating CLAUDE.md companion
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @keshavsuki on fixing CLAUDE.md staleness: add a primer.md that Claude rewrites at end of every session (current state, next step, blockers), plus a memory.sh launch hook that injects last 5 commits and modified files, plus a git hook logging commits. Four-layer always-current context.
- Entries: DVzvsgijOLT, DLuiACnKWP9, DVQ5o6YDfsp, DVKJlJYEsmm, DV6QY8QjoRW, DV3fvORksud

## 7 must-install Claude Code repos — Obsidian Skills, n8n-MCP, Superpowers
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @chase.h.ai lists 7 Claude Code repos with GitHub links: kepano/obsidian-skills, czlonkowski/n8n-mcp, nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill, thedotmack/claude-mem, glittercowboy/get-shit-done, hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code, obra/superpowers. High-signal resource dump.
- Entries: DUzg0n1jRK-, DLuiACnKWP9, DHoPsMOxIRl, DVQ5o6YDfsp, DVKJlJYEsmm, DV6QY8QjoRW

## Boris Cherny — 10-15 parallel Claude sessions + evolving CLAUDE.md
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @mindwired.ai summary of Boris Cherny's workflow: 10-15 Claude sessions in parallel across terminal and web, CLAUDE.md continuously updated after mistakes as reusable guardrails, Claude-CLI for data queries. Notes Claude Code's share of public GitHub activity.
- Entries: DVMmXlME6mZ, DLuiACnKWP9, DVQ5o6YDfsp, DVKJlJYEsmm, DV6QY8QjoRW, DV3fvORksud

## Polymarket weather edge — dorm weather station, $100K+ traded
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @insidesteal profile of a student generating $100K+ on Polymarket weather contracts using a dorm-built weather station and localized data asymmetry. Reference for prediction-market quant plays built on proprietary data.
- Entries: DWzz7T9Ddex, DWEfIS9jRgI, DWiFum7j-cS, DUI845-k3WR, DUrQ187EvI4, DTZyMVPiHM5

## llms.txt — get ChatGPT to recommend your local business
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @brycenwood.ai got ChatGPT to recommend his vehicle-wrap shop via an llms.txt file (built in 5min with Claude Code) describing business, location, value prop. Early GEO (generative engine optimization) tactic.
- Entries: DWxmSwTDFk2, DOPqBfyDMHG, DNTuDtvtZkS, DLuiACnKWP9, DLTpkBSoiwX, DGn-XM2vikd

## Onyx — open-source self-hosted agentic RAG platform
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @marc.kaz flags onyx-dot-app/onyx hitting #1 GitHub trending: self-hostable AI platform with agentic RAG, deep research mode, custom agents, web+code execution, voice/image gen, 50+ connectors. Strong self-hosted AI stack option.
- Entries: DWtgbzLDgwm, DLuiACnKWP9, DVQ5o6YDfsp, DV6QY8QjoRW, DVmBYgnDzp2, DWzHY9lkSZS

## Claude Code 'brevity mandate' — leaked system prompt tradeoff
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @gojutechtalk on the leaked Claude Code system prompt's 'brevity mandate' rule that pushes CC to minimize code changes, which he argues leads to spaghetti-on-spaghetti fixes. Useful critique to remember.
- Entries: DWoYMJDD6oO, DOPqBfyDMHG, DNTuDtvtZkS, DLuiACnKWP9, DLTpkBSoiwX, DGn-XM2vikd

## 10 non-cliche quant research projects — Bayesian BL, RL factors, PBO
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @heidioftheeast's 10 quant project ideas beyond Black-Scholes: probability of backtest overfitting, Bayesian Black-Litterman, survival-based risk, RL factor portfolios, Sharpe statistics, convex portfolio trading, TS vs XS factors, numeraire-invariant hedging, exotic option model risk, regime-switching factors.
- Entries: DTZyMVPiHM5, DWzz7T9Ddex, DWiFum7j-cS, DUI845-k3WR, DUrQ187EvI4, DUytAorCGE_

## 10 institutional quant projects — Avellaneda-Stoikov, Hawkes, HMM
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @heidioftheeast's deeper 10-project list: Avellaneda-Stoikov market maker, multivariate Hawkes order-flow, Almgren-Chriss cross-impact, eigenportfolios via random matrix theory, realized-kernel vol forecasting, meta-labeling, orthogonal alpha stacking, HMM regime detection, adversarial stress tests, capacity/impact scaling. Serious portfolio builder.
- Entries: DUytAorCGE_, DWzz7T9Ddex, DWiFum7j-cS, DUI845-k3WR, DUrQ187EvI4, DTZyMVPiHM5

## Anthropic Claude Code source-code leak — scale commentary
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @gojutechtalk framing the Anthropic Claude Code source leak as potentially the largest source-code leak ever. Reference for 'the leak' as a discussion point.
- Entries: DWmQYXikbqq, DLuiACnKWP9, DVQ5o6YDfsp, DVKJlJYEsmm, DV6QY8QjoRW, DV3fvORksud

## Claude Code hooks — 3 daily-driver hooks from @jens.heitmann
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @jens.heitmann on Claude Code hooks being under-utilized, with his 3 daily hooks. Pointer to Jens's CC hooks content; specifics live in the video.
- Entries: DV8rkpGEbBJ, DLuiACnKWP9, DVQ5o6YDfsp, DVKJlJYEsmm, DV6QY8QjoRW, DV3fvORksud

## Polymarket 99c limit-order edge — 75 automated trades/hour
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @beinginvested on a Polymarket trader placing limit orders on outcomes already closing at 99c, automated at 75 trades/hour, 4 months to crack it. Teaser with DM-gated full breakdown; worth remembering the pattern.
- Entries: DWEfIS9jRgI, DWzz7T9Ddex, DUrQ187EvI4, DUoiJH_EiqP

## Hunyuan3D-2 — Tencent's free image-to-3D model generator
- Class: guide
- Thesis: Tencent's Hunyuan3D-2 turns text or an uploaded image into textured 3D models in seconds. Free web UI; pipeline is upload > Generate Shape > Generate Textured Shape > download. Good for game/app asset workflows.
- Entries: DIcw6N6snq1, DD5q3AcS8KS, DDF0mC1vvkh

## World Labs — explorable 3D worlds from a single 2D image
- Class: guide
- Thesis: @rpn covers World Labs' first demo: generate navigable 3D worlds from one image or sentence, with camera controls and style effects. Referenced as a likely Quest/Vision Pro use case.
- Entries: DDF0mC1vvkh, DIcw6N6snq1, DD5q3AcS8KS

## music — 8 posts
